grammar_tips.title: Putting It All Together
grammar_tips.explanation: You now know all six forms of essere, how to give your name, say where you're from, and introduce others. This lesson is pure practice — no new grammar. By the end, you should be able to handle a basic Italian introduction without thinking twice.

grammar_tips.title: Formal vs Informal Introductions
grammar_tips.explanation: With friends: 'Ciao, mi chiamo Marco. Sono di Roma.' With a stranger: 'Buongiorno, sono Marco. Sono di Roma.' The content is almost the same — it's the greeting that sets the register. 'Mi chiamo' works in both, but 'Sono + name' feels slightly more formal.

grammar_tips.title: Piacere — Nice to Meet You
grammar_tips.explanation: 'Piacere' means 'nice to meet you' (literally: 'pleasure'). Say it after someone introduces themselves. It works in any register — formal or informal. You can also say 'Piacere mio' (the pleasure is mine) to be extra polite.

prompt.text: What does 'piacere' mean when meeting someone?
Which is the correct informal introduction?

hints: Informal = 'ciao' people. Just name, no title.

prompt.text: Complete the sentence with the missing word.
Someone introduces themselves. Say 'nice to meet you' in Italian.
How do you ask 'Where are you from?' informally?

hints: Three words: from + where + you are.

prompt.text: How do you say 'I am a teacher' in Italian?

hints: Two words — no article needed with professions.

prompt.text: Arrange the words: 'Hello, my name is Anna. Nice to meet you!'
Arrange the words: 'This is Paolo. He is a doctor.'

exercises.sentence_context: Questo è Paolo. Lui è dottore.
hints: Introduce him first (this is...), then say what he does.

prompt.text: Arrange the words: 'Good morning, I am Doctor Rossi. I am from Venice.'

exercises.sentence_context: Buongiorno, sono il dottor Rossi. Sono di Venezia.
hints: Formal greeting, then title + name, then origin.

exercises.sentence_context: Buongiorno! Mi chiamo Anna. Sono di Firenze. Sono insegnante. Piacere!
exercises.correct_answer: Buongiorno! Mi chiamo Anna. Sono di Firenze. Sono insegnante. Piacere!
hints: A full formal introduction: greeting, name, city, profession, nice to meet you.

exercises.sentence_context: Ciao! Mi chiamo Luca. Di dove sei?
exercises.correct_answer: Ciao! Mi chiamo Luca. Di dove sei?
hints: An informal introduction followed by a question.
